findings.description: Critical: `wp-coordinator-migrate` still tells the implementer that `railway.toml` needs no changes, while D8 and task 4.5 require adding a `[build] buildCommand`. An implementation agent following the work-package description can skip the Railway prebuild and reintroduce the production Docker failure.
findings.resolution: Update the `wp-coordinator-migrate.description` to say docker-compose.yml remains unchanged but `agent-coordinator/railway.toml` must gain the concrete `[build] buildCommand`; keep the existing locks/write_allow entries for railway.toml.

findings.description: Critical: The planned CI wheel prebuild command is not executable in the existing docker-smoke job: the job defaults `working-directory` to `agent-coordinator`, but the plan says to run `cd packages/gen-eval`, and the job currently has no `astral-sh/setup-uv` step. That means the CI Docker build may still run without `agent-coordinator/dist/gen_eval-*.whl`.
findings.resolution: In D8 and `wp-package-tests`, require the docker-smoke job to add `uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v5` before the prebuild and run the wheel build from a valid directory, either with `working-directory: .` plus `cd packages/gen-eval && uv build --out-dir ../../agent-coordinator/dist` or from `agent-coordinator` with `cd ../packages/gen-eval && uv build --out-dir ../../agent-coordinator/dist`.

findings.description: Critical: The existing GitHub Actions docker smoke import list still imports `evaluation.gen_eval.mcp_service`, but the plan does not explicitly update that CI string. After the legacy module is removed, the smoke-import step will fail even if the package extraction itself works.
findings.resolution: Add to task 3.4 or wp-package-tests that `.github/workflows/ci.yml` must change the smoke module from `evaluation.gen_eval.mcp_service` to `gen_eval.mcp_service`, and add a repo or CI-specific grep that catches legacy `evaluation.gen_eval` strings outside the moved OpenSpec artifacts.

findings.description: Nit: Task 5.C now checks both dotted and slash legacy forms, but `wp-skills-update` verification still only greps `evaluation.gen_eval`. The slash-path regression that motivated the round-1 fix would not be caught by the work-package verification step.
findings.resolution: Mirror task 5.C in `wp-skills-update.verification.steps`: run both `grep -r 'evaluation\.gen_eval' ...` and `grep -r 'evaluation/gen_eval' ...` across `skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, and `.agents/skills/`.

findings.description: The round-1 fixes are otherwise present in the core design/tasks: D5/D8 now mandate `--no-sources`, task 7.4 uses the `agent-coordinator/` Docker context with a prior wheel build, task 5.2 updates the descriptor glob, and the stale `deny` block was removed.
findings.resolution: No action required for those fixed items; keep the corrected design/task language during implementation.
